items.title: 'Serious breach' revealed by Pakistani police after Australian girl shot

children.content: Pakistani officials apologise for the fatal shooting of a nine-year-old Australian girl, and outline the violation of protocols that led to her death as they pursue murder charges against the officer involved.

gallery.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

thumbnail.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

items.title: Aboriginal interpreters call for police engagement after inquest finding

children.content: More than a decade after Gene Gibson was wrongfully convicted for the murder of Broome tradesman Josh Warneke, Aboriginal interpreters say they are still overlooked by police.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat stands in front of a sign for an Aboriginal interpreting service.

items.title: Authorities tape up 50 Perth stores in biggest illegal tobacco blitz under new laws

children.content: Authorities say a recent two-week compliance blitz results in them seizing $11 million of illegal product like cigarettes and vapes, but how much is it disrupting the lucrative industry?

image.altText: Shop with glass doors covered in large Store Closed signs

items.title: 13-year-old's death prompted promises of reform. Her mother says little has changed

children.content: The mother of Kate Savage, whose death at 13 sparked a review of mental health supports for children, says she's lost faith promises of reform will ever be kept.

gallery.altText: A young girl poses for a close up photo
Kate smiles at the camera. She is on a boat, in front of what appears to be a castle or fort.
A woman looking to the left of frame with a serious expression.

thumbnail.altText: A young girl poses for a close up photo
Kate smiles at the camera. She is on a boat, in front of what appears to be a castle or fort.
A woman looking to the left of frame with a serious expression.

items.title: Gina Rinehart's iron ore company flags job losses, reportedly in the hundreds

children.content: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore has cut jobs a year after merging its two iron ore businesses into a single entity, but won't say how many.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart stands at a podium.

items.title: WA rail union pushes back on proposal for safety lights on trains

children.content: The state's union for train drivers says a draft bill mandating more lights on trains is "fundamentally flawed" and would not fix motorists' behaviour at level crossings.

image.altText: A WA railway line with a large stop look for trains picture in front.

items.title: Police say man thankful after drug arrest halts lethal mushroom mix-up

children.content: A man dodges potentially fatal poisoning after allegedly mistaking lethal death cap mushrooms for their hallucinogenic counterparts while illegally foraging in southern Western Australia.

gallery.altText: Supplied photo of a poisonous mushroom seized by WA Police.

thumbnail.altText: Supplied photo of a poisonous mushroom seized by WA Police.
A yellow-tinged mushroom rests on the ground among leaves and twigs.

items.title: Mother of murdered Broome man slams WA Police culture

children.content: Ingrid Bishop says poor culture within WA Police hampered the investigation into her son Josh Warneke's death from the start.

articles.title: Tradie found dead by side of road 16 years ago was murdered, coroner finds

children.content: A coroner has found the investigation into a Broome tradies' murder, which remains an enduring mystery, was mishandled by WA Police.

image.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

gallery.altText: A woman in a floral shirt speaks to reporters outside court
Bo wearing white shirt, smiling with hands to face and long hair
Young man with blonde hair looking into camera

Bo wearing white shirt, smiling with hands to face and long hair

items.title: WA social housing waitlist to be overhauled for first time in 70 years

image.altText: A close up of John Carey delivering a press conference

children.content: The WA government announces plans to undertake the first reform of the social housing waitlist in more than 70 years, with families fleeing domestic violence and homeless people to be among those prioritised for housing.

items.title: ASIC launches Zone RV criminal investigation over caravan company's collapse

children.content: The former director of Zone RV faces a criminal probe into allegations he acted recklessly or dishonestly before the manufacturer collapsed with debts of $42 million.

image.altText: caravans in a row

items.title: CFMEU accused of 'dark strategy' to generate millions in 'illegitimate' revenue

children.content: An inquiry into alleged union misconduct and criminal links in Queensland hears the CFMEU weaponised workplace agreements to bolster its revenue.

image.altText: Exterior photos of a CFMEU building.

items.title: New Zealanders become first World Cup mother-son duo

children.content: When Tyler Bindon came off the bench for New Zealand in its men's World Cup opener against Iran, he made a bit of history with his mother.

articles.title: England enjoying 'right to dream' about winning World Cup

children.content: As a World Cup favourite, England is pushing aside decades of heartbreak and disappointment to embrace the pressure of finally trying to bring football home again.

image.altText: England players train together in Dallas at 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: The night Scotland painted Boston's baseball heartland in tartan

children.content: Throughout the famed but understated bleachers of Boston's Fenway Park, an army of Scottish football fans created a night few fans will ever forget.

image.altText: GettyImages-2281132655

gallery.altText: A composite image of Tyler and Jenny Bindon.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of Tyler and Jenny Bindon.

items.title: One Nation prepared to cut worker entitlements to shore up struggling small businesses

children.content: One Nation's newly named "treasury spokesman" Barnaby Joyce says the party is prepared to cut worker entitlements to safeguard small businesses struggling to deal with inflation and high power prices.

image.altText: A man and a woman stand next to each other.

items.title: 'I didn't have a life': Pensioner moves to Thailand for cheaper living

children.content: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

image.altText: A selfie of a smiling man in silver-rimmed glasses and a grey tee taken from inside a restauraunt with red lanterns.

items.title: Tree advocates dismayed as Melville council backtracks on canopy protection

children.content: Plans to protect established trees on private property have had a setback in Perth's southern suburbs, with councillors concerned that protections may go too far.

image.altText: Two skyrise buildings with green trees in the foreground.

items.title: What happened when Ann Patchett quit swearing for good

children.content: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

image.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

items.title: El Niño has arrived and it could be the most volatile yet

children.content: El Niño is declared and scientists say the climate change is making its impacts harder to predict.

image.altText: A man in boots standing on dusty, dry land.

items.title: After 11 years on a housing wait list, Lynette is getting desperate

children.content: As the number of people waiting for social housing continues to grow, the amount of available homes is falling as a proportion of Australia's total housing stock.

image.altText: Woman wearing glasses sitting on a red couch

items.title: 'Extraordinary waste': Accused criminals flown from remote areas as courts close

children.content: WA Police have been forced to fly some accused criminals out of remote communities since the start of this month due to regional court closures sparked by a security dispute.

gallery.altText: a small brick courthouse with two benches outside

thumbnail.altText: a small brick courthouse with two benches outside

items.title: Six gaming experts share their favourite video games of the year so far

children.content: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

image.altText: Three people shaped characters stand with a pokemon trubbish

items.title: Bluey speaks Indigenous language in time for NAIDOC week

children.content: The animated award-winning cartoon series Bluey has been translated into Yolŋu Matha, from north-east Arnhem Land, for NAIDOC Week.

image.altText: Bluey is sitting at the beach playing with a shell.

items.title: Government announces $500m in sports funding over next two years

children.content: The $513 million two-year package will include cost of living assistance for athletes, funding for dozens of sporting programs, and boosts for Winter and Paralympic athletes.

image.altText: A runner crouches at the starting line holding a baton.

items.title: What a trillion dollars costs democracy

children.content: Democracy cannot survive trillionaires. We need to start taxing billionaires properly.

image.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.

items.title: Wharfie Time or crunch time, these are the AFL teams coming up clutch

children.content: The Dockers use Wharfie Time to get a big boost from their fans, while everyone else toils away in what they call crunch time. In an AFL season full of tight finishes, which teams are holding their nerve best and why?

gallery.altText: An AFL player in purple has his arms outstretched celebrating a goal
